---
title: "The Kone (Quin Konehead Pre-Memorial Route)"
featuredImage: "https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/06/111224053_large_14943434451.qy3bJqfs.jpg"
miles: 1.6
gain: 785
highestElevation: 2807
classRating: "class5"
ydsRating: "9"
ydsSubRating: "none"
pitches: 5
parentArea: content/areas/three-o-clock-rock.mdx
---

## Time estimates

C2C: 4.5 hours including 20 mins of transition time

- Approach: 40 mins or less
- Climbing: 2 hours (25 min/pitch)
- Rappel: 40 mins (4 rappels with single 70m)

## P1 - 5.9, 5 bolts

Climb up the middle of the sweeping slab between left facing flakes. After the first bolt, place a **#0.5 cam** in the crack before reaching the second bolt. Aim for the left side of an overlap. Clip the last bolt below the overhang, then pass it on the right.

<RouteOverlay 
  imageSrc="https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/09/IMG_5636.5jo02Nk5-768x1024.jpg"

;is&quot;:1.0542857142857143,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;x&quot;:368,&quot;y&quot;:224,&quot;w&quot;:10,&quot;h&quot;:10,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;&quot;}]}"

![Top of P1](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/09/IMG_5638.SzFs1SY4-768x1024.jpg)

## P2 - 5.5, 3 bolts

A short pitch leads left and up past three bolts to an anchor beneath the left of two overlaps.

<RouteOverlay 
  imageSrc="https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/09/IMG_5639.JfPfK4_-768x1024.jpg"

  topoOverlaySrc="data:image/png;base64,"
/>

## P3 - 5.9, 3 bolts

Pass the overlap to the left (7) or straight up through the middle (9). Follow knobby face to the next anchor).

![The 5.9 start](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/06/111224018_large_14943434451.3NLypjeP-768x1024.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/06/106813292_large_14941427641.a4szLGx9-1024x768.jpg)

## P4 - 5.9, 6 bolts

Climb up and left along the face and under a large roof past six more bolts to a good stance. At the start, a medium size nut/cam just after the first bolt can optionally be placed.

![Start of P4](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/06/111224053_large_14943434451.9oYTacjM-768x1024.jpg)
![Climbers on P4](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/06/111771286_large_14943035421.FwuIhvwP-1024x768.jpg)
![End of P4](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/06/106813293_large_14941427651.g7PGqseg-1024x768.jpg)

## P5 - 5.8, 5 bolts

Climb the fun, and easier, slab up and left past five bolts to the final anchors.

![Looking down P5](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/06/106813294_large_14941427661.x8dGXV9G-1024x768.jpg)

## Descent

Can rap with single 70m now by using the Magic Bus anchors (climbers left and slightly down from Tidbits anchors).
